steps.title: Identify Bartley's claim about misfortune
steps.content: Bartley says that any misfortune that comes to the world lands on him, setting up the point that needs emphasis.

steps.title: Match the simile's purpose to the claim
steps.content:
The underlined simile likens his misfortune to a flock of crows descending on seed potatoes, creating a vivid picture that reinforces his statement.

The other three choices either describe Mrs. Tarpey’s view, frame it as a counterargument, or treat it as a witnessed scene, none of which match the simile’s role.

That matches choice B — B is correct.
